 900\n41\t}\n42\tfn default_max_sessions() -> u32 {\n43\t    100_000\n44\t}\n45\tfn default_wait_strategy() -> String {\n46\t    \"block\".into()\n47\t}\n48\tfn default_max_wait() -> u64 {\n49\t         max_sessions: default_max_sessions(),\n58\t            wait_strategy: default_wait_strategy(),\n59\t            max_wait_ms: default_max_wait(),\n60\t        }\n61\t    }\n62\t}\n63\t\n64\timpl From<crate::config::advanced::SessionPinningConfig> for SessionPinningConfig {\n65\t    fn from(config: crate::config::advanced::SessionPinningConfig) -> Self {\n66\t        Self {\n67\t            enabled: config.enabled,\n68\t            ttl_seconds: config.ttl_seconds,\n69\t            max_sessions: config.max_sessions,\n70\t            wait_strategy: struct SessionState {\n79\t    /// Last write miroir task ID (if any).\n80\t    pub last_write_mtask_id: Option<String>,\n81\t    /// Last write timestamp.\n82\t    pub last_write_at: u64,\n83\t    /// Pinned replica group ID.\n84\t    pub pinned_group: Option<u32>,\n85\t    /// Minimum settings version observed by this session.\n86\t    pub min_settings_version: u64,\n87\t    /// Session created at.\n88\t    pub created_at: u64,\n89\t    /// Session expires at.\n90\t    pub expires_at: u64,\n91\t}\n92\t\n93\timpl SessionState {\n94\t    /// Check if this session is expired.\n95\t    pub fn is_expired(&self) -> bool {\n96\t        let now = millis_now();\n97\t        now > self.expires_at\n98\t    }\n99\t\n100\t    /// Check if there's a pending write.\n101\t    pub fn has_pending_write(&self) -> bool {\n102\t        self.last_write_mtask_id.is_some()\n103\t    }\n104\t}\n105\t\n106\t/// Session pinning manager.\n107\tpub struct SessionManager {\n108\t    /// Configuration.\n109\t    config: SessionPinningConfig,\n110\t    /// Session ID -> Session state (IndexMap maintains insertion order for LRU).\n111\t    sessions: Arc<RwLock<IndexMap<String, SessionState>>>,\n112\t    /// Per-index pending writes (session_id -> mtask_id).\n113\t    pending_writes: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, HashMap<String, String>>>>,\n114\t}\n115\t\n116\timpl SessionManager {\n117\t    /// Create a new session manager.\n118\t    pub fn new(config: SessionPinningConfig) -> Self {\n119\t        Self {\n120\t            config,\n121\t            sessions: Arc::new(RwLock::new(IndexMap::new())),\n122\t            pending_writes: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n123\t        }\n124\t    }\n125\t\n126\t    /// Record a write for a session.\n127\t    ///\n128\t    /// Returns the group ID that was pinned (first to reach quorum).\n129\t    ///\n130\t    /// This method should be called AFTER per-group quorum is achieved,\n131\t    /// with the `first_quorum_group` being the first group to reach quorum.\n132\t    pub async fn record_write_with_quorum(\n133\t        &self,\n134\t        session_id: &str,\n135\t        mtask_id: String,\n136\t        first_quorum_group: u32,\n137\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n138\t        if !self.config.enabled {\n139\t            return Ok(());\n140\t        }\n141\t\n142\t        let now = millis_now();\n143\t        let expires_at = now + (self.config.ttl_seconds * 1000);\n144\t\n145\t        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n146\t\n147\t        // Enforce max sessions (simple FIFO - remove oldest entry when at capacity)\n148\t        if sessions.len() >= self.config.max_sessions as usize {\n149\t            // Remove oldest entry (first key in HashMap)\n150\t            if let Some(key) = sessions.keys().next().cloned() {\n151\t                sessions.remove(&key);\n152\t                debug!(session_id = %key, \"evicted oldest session to enforce max_sessions\");\n153\t            }\n154\t        }\n155\t\n156\t        // Get or create session\n157\t        let session = sessions.entry(session_id.to_string()).or_insert(SessionState {\n158\t            last_write_mtask_id: None,\n159\t            last_write_at: 0,\n160\t            pinned_group: None,\n161\t            min_settings_version: 0,\n162\t            created_at: now,\n163\t            expires_at,\n164\t        });\n165\t\n166\t        // Update session state\n167\t        session.last_write_mtask_id = Some(mtask_id.clone());\n168\t        session.last_write_at = now;\n169\t        session.expires_at = expires_at;\n170\t\n171\t        // Pin the group if not already pinned (first write wins)\n172\t        if session.pinned_group.is_none() {\n173\t            session.pinned_group = Some(first_quorum_group);\n174\t            info!(\n175\t                session_id = %session_id,\n176\t                mtask_id = %mtask_id,\n177\t                pinned_group = first_quorum_group,\n178\t                \"session pinned to first-quorum group\"\n179\t            );\n180\t        } else {\n181\t            debug!(\n182\t                session_id = %session_id,\n183\t                existing_group = session.pinned_group,\n184\t                new_quorum_group = first_quorum_group,\n185\t                \"session already pinned, ignoring new quorum group\"\n186\t            );\n187\t        }\n188\t\n189\t        // Track pending write per session\n190\t        let mut pending = self.pending_writes.write().await;\n191\t        pending.entry(session_id.to_string())\n192\t            .or_insert_with(HashMap::new)\n193\t            .insert(mtask_id.clone(), first_quorum_group.to_string());\n194\t\n195\t        Ok(())\n196\t    }\n197\t\n198\t    /// Wait for a pending write to complete (block strategy).\n199\t    ///\n200\t    /// Polls the task registry until the write succeeds or times out.\n201\t    pub async fn wait_for_write_completion<T: TaskRegistry + ?Sized>(\n202\t        &self,\n203\t        session_id: &str,\n204\t        task_registry: &Arc<T>,\n205\t        max_wait: Duration,\n206\t    ) -> Result<bool> {\n207\t        let session = {\n208\t            let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n209\t            sessions.get(session_id).cloned()\n210\t        };\n211\t\n212\t        let session = session.ok_or_else(|| {\n213\t            MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"session not found\".to_string())\n214\t        })?;\n215\t\n216\t        let mtask_id = session.last_write_mtask_id.ok_or_else(|| {\n217\t            MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"no pending write for session\".to_string())\n218\t        })?;\n219\t\n220\t        let start = SystemTime::now();\n221\t        let mut poll_delay = 25; // Start with 25ms\n222\t\n223\t        loop {\n224\t            // Check task status\n225\t            if let Ok(Some(task)) = task_registry.get(&mtask_id) {\n226\t                match task.status {\n227\t                    TaskStatus::Succeeded => {\n228\t                        // Clear pending write state\n229\t                        self.clear_pending_write(session_id).await;\n230\t                        return Ok(true);\n231\t                    }\n232\t                    TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled => {\n233\t                        // Clear pending write state even on failure\n234\t                        self.clear_pending_write(session_id).await;\n235\t                        return Ok(false);\n236\t                    }\n237\t                    _ => {}\n238\t                }\n239\t            }\n240\t\n241\t            // Check timeout\n242\t            let elapsed = start.elapsed().unwrap_or(Duration::ZERO);\n243\t            if elapsed >= max_wait {\n244\t                warn!(\n245\t                    session_id = %session_id,\n246\t                    mtask_id = %mtask_id,\n247\t                    elapsed_ms = elapsed.as_millis(),\n248\t                    max_wait_ms = max_wait.as_millis(),\n249\t                    \"session pin wait timeout\"\n250\t                );\n251\t                return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"session pin wait timeout\".to_string()));\n252\t            }\n253\t\n254\t            // Exponential backoff with cap\n255\t            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(poll_delay)).await;\n256\t            poll_delay = std::cmp::min(poll_delay * 2, 500); // Cap at 500ms\n257\t        }\n258\t    }\n259\t\n260\t    /// Get the pinned group for a session (if any).\n261\t    ///\n262\t    /// Returns None if:\n263\t    /// - Session doesn't exist\n264\t    /// - Session has no pending write\n265\t    /// - Session is expired\n266\t    pub async fn get_pinned_group(&self, session_id: &str) -> Option<u32> {\n267\t        if !self.config.enabled {\n268\t            return None;\n269\t        }\n270\t\n271\t        let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n272\t        let session = sessions.get(session_id)?;\n273\t\n274\t        if session.is_expired() {\n275\t            return None;\n276\t        }\n277\t\n278\t        if !session.has_pending_write() {\n279\t            return None;\n280\t        }\n281\t\n282\t        session.pinned_group\n283\t    }\n284\t\n285\t    /// Clear the pending write state for a session.\n286\t    ///\n287\t    /// Called when the write task completes.\n288\t    pub async fn clear_pending_write(&self, session_id: &str) {\n289\t        let mut pending = self.pending_writes.write().await;\n290\t        pending.remove(session_id);\n291\t\n292\t        // Also clear the last_write_mtask_id in the session\n293\t        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n294\t        if let Some(session) = sessions.get_mut(session_id) {\n295\t            session.last_write_mtask_id = None;\n296\t        }\n297\t    }\n298\t\n299\t    /// Get session state.\n300\t    pub async fn get_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> Option<SessionState> {\n301\t        let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n302\t        sessions.get(session_id).cloned()\n303\t    }\n304\t\n305\t    /// Delete a session.\n306\t    pub async fn delete_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> bool {\n307\t        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n308\t        let mut pending = self.pending_writes.write().await;\n309\t        pending.remove(session_id);\n310\t        sessions.remove(session_id).is_some()\n311\t    }\n312\t\n313\t    /// Clean up expired sessions.\n314\t    pub async fn prune_expired(&self) -> usize {\n315\t        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n316\t        let mut pending = self.pending_writes.write().await;\n317\t\n318\t        let now = millis_now();\n319\t        let mut to_remove = Vec::new();\n320\t\n321\t        for (id, session) in sessions.iter() {\n322\t            if session.is_expired() {\n323\t                to_remove.push(id.clone());\n324\t            }\n325\t        }\n326\t\n327\t        for id in &to_remove {\n328\t            sessions.remove(id);\n329\t            pending.remove(id);\n330\t        }\n331\t\n332\t        to_remove.len()\n333\t    }\n334\t\n335\t    /// Get current session count.\n336\t    pub async fn session_count(&self) -> usize {\n337\t        let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n338\t        sessions.len()\n339\t    }\n340\t\n341\t    /// Handle pinned group failure - clear the pin for a session.\n342\t    ///\n343\t    /// Called when the pinned group for a session becomes unavailable.\n344\t    /// Subsequent reads will use normal routing.\n345\t    pub async fn handle_pinned_group_failure(&self, session_id: &str, failed_group: u32) -> bool {\n346\t        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n347\t        if let Some(session) = sessions.get_mut(session_id) {\n348\t            if session.pinned_group == Some(failed_group) {\n349\t                info!(\n350\t                    session_id = %session_id,\n351\t                    failed_group,\n352\t                    \"clearing session pin due to group failure\"\n353\t                );\n354\t                session.pinned_group = None;\n355\t                // Also clear pending write state since we can't guarantee visibility\n356\t                session.last_write_mtask_id = None;\n357\t                return true;\n358\t            }\n359\t        }\n360\t        false\n361\t    }\n362\t\n363\t    /// Get the wait strategy.\n364\t    pub fn wait_strategy(&self) -> WaitStrategy {\n365\t        match self.config.wait_strategy.as_str() {\n366\t            \"block\" => WaitStrategy::Block,\n367\t            \"route_pin\" => WaitStrategy::RoutePin,\n368\t            _ => WaitStrategy::Block,\n369\t        }\n370\t    }\n371\t\n372\t    /// Get max wait Session pinning for read-your-writes consistency (plan §13.6).\n//!\n//! Clients provide X-Miroir-Session header; Miroir tracks pending writes\n//! and routes subsequent reads to the pinned replica group.\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::task::{TaskRegistry, TaskStatus};\nuse indexmap::IndexMap;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn};\n\n/// Session pinning configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SessionPinningConfig {\n    /// Whether session pinning is enabled.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    /// Session TTL in seconds.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_ttl\")]\n    pub ttl_seconds: u64,\n    /// Maximum number of sessions.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_max_sessions\")]\n    pub max_sessions: u32,\n    /// Wait strategy: \"block\" or \"route_pin\".\n    #[serde(default = \"default_wait_strategy\")]\n    pub wait_strategy: String,\n    /// Maximum wait time in milliseconds.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_max_wait\")]\n    pub max_wait_ms: u64,\n}\n\nfn default_true() -> bool {\n    true\n}\nfn default_ttl() -> u64 {\n    900\n}\nfn default_max_sessions() -> u32 {\n    100_000\n}\nfn default_wait_strategy() -> String {\n    \"block\".into()\n}\nfn default_max_wait() -> u64 {\n    5000\n}\n\nimpl Default for SessionPinningConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            ttl_seconds: default_ttl(),\n            max_sessions: default_max_sessions(),\n            wait_strategy: default_wait_strategy(),\n            max_wait_ms: default_max_wait(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<crate::config::advanced::SessionPinningConfig> for SessionPinningConfig {\n    fn from(config: crate::config::advanced::SessionPinningConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: config.enabled,\n            ttl_seconds: config.ttl_seconds,\n            max_sessions: config.max_sessions,\n            wait_strategy: config.wait_strategy,\n            max_wait_ms: config.max_wait_ms,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Session state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SessionState {\n    /// Last write miroir task ID (if any).\n    pub last_write_mtask_id: Option<String>,\n    /// Last write timestamp.\n    pub last_write_at: u64,\n    /// Pinned replica group ID.\n    pub pinned_group: Option<u32>,\n    /// Minimum settings version observed by this session.\n    pub min_settings_version: u64,\n    /// Session created at.\n    pub created_at: u64,\n    /// Session expires at.\n    pub expires_at: u64,\n}\n\nimpl SessionState {\n    /// Check if this session is expired.\n    pub fn is_expired(&self) -> bool {\n        let now = millis_now();\n        now > self.expires_at\n    }\n\n    /// Check if there's a pending write.\n    pub fn has_pending_write(&self) -> bool {\n        self.last_write_mtask_id.is_some()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Session pinning manager.\npub struct SessionManager {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: SessionPinningConfig,\n    /// Session ID -> Session state (IndexMap maintains insertion order for LRU).\n    sessions: Arc<RwLock<IndexMap<String, SessionState>>>,\n    /// Per-index pending writes (session_id -> mtask_id).\n    pending_writes: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, HashMap<String, String>>>>,\n}\n\nimpl SessionManager {\n    /// Create a new session manager.\n    pub fn new(config: SessionPinningConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            sessions: Arc::new(RwLock::new(IndexMap::new())),\n            pending_writes: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Record a write for a session.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the group ID that was pinned (first to reach quorum).\n    ///\n    /// This method should be called AFTER per-group quorum is achieved,\n    /// with the `first_quorum_group` being the first group to reach quorum.\n    pub async fn record_write_with_quorum(\n        &self,\n        session_id: &str,\n        mtask_id: String,\n        first_quorum_group: u32,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        if !self.config.enabled {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        let now = millis_now();\n        let expires_at = now + (self.config.ttl_seconds * 1000);\n\n        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n\n        // Enforce max sessions (simple FIFO - remove oldest entry when at capacity)\n        if sessions.len() >= self.config.max_sessions as usize {\n            // Remove oldest entry (first key in HashMap)\n            if let Some(key) = sessions.keys().next().cloned() {\n                sessions.remove(&key);\n                debug!(session_id = %key, \"evicted oldest session to enforce max_sessions\");\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Get or create session\n        let session = sessions.entry(session_id.to_string()).or_insert(SessionState {\n            last_write_mtask_id: None,\n            last_write_at: 0,\n            pinned_group: None,\n            min_settings_version: 0,\n            created_at: now,\n            expires_at,\n        });\n\n        // Update session state\n        session.last_write_mtask_id = Some(mtask_id.clone());\n        session.last_write_at = now;\n        session.expires_at = expires_at;\n\n        // Pin the group if not already pinned (first write wins)\n        if session.pinned_group.is_none() {\n            session.pinned_group = Some(first_quorum_group);\n            info!(\n                session_id = %session_id,\n                mtask_id = %mtask_id,\n                pinned_group = first_quorum_group,\n                \"session pinned to first-quorum group\"\n            );\n        } else {\n            debug!(\n                session_id = %session_id,\n                existing_group = session.pinned_group,\n                new_quorum_group = first_quorum_group,\n                \"session already pinned, ignoring new quorum group\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Track pending write per session\n        let mut pending = self.pending_writes.write().await;\n        pending.entry(session_id.to_string())\n            .or_insert_with(HashMap::new)\n            .insert(mtask_id.clone(), first_quorum_group.to_string());\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Wait for a pending write to complete (block strategy).\n    ///\n    /// Polls the task registry until the write succeeds or times out.\n    pub async fn wait_for_write_completion<T: TaskRegistry + ?Sized>(\n        &self,\n        session_id: &str,\n        task_registry: &Arc<T>,\n        max_wait: Duration,\n    ) -> Result<bool> {\n        let session = {\n            let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n            sessions.get(session_id).cloned()\n        };\n\n        let session = session.ok_or_else(|| {\n            MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"session not found\".to_string())\n        })?;\n\n        let mtask_id = session.last_write_mtask_id.ok_or_else(|| {\n            MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"no pending write for session\".to_string())\n        })?;\n\n        let start = SystemTime::now();\n        let mut poll_delay = 25; // Start with 25ms\n\n        loop {\n            // Check task status\n            if let Ok(Some(task)) = task_registry.get(&mtask_id) {\n                match task.status {\n                    TaskStatus::Succeeded => {\n                        // Clear pending write state\n                        self.clear_pending_write(session_id).await;\n                        return Ok(true);\n                    }\n                    TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled => {\n                        // Clear pending write state even on failure\n                        self.clear_pending_write(session_id).await;\n                        return Ok(false);\n                    }\n                    _ => {}\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Check timeout\n            let elapsed = start.elapsed().unwrap_or(Duration::ZERO);\n            if elapsed >= max_wait {\n                warn!(\n                    session_id = %session_id,\n                    mtask_id = %mtask_id,\n                    elapsed_ms = elapsed.as_millis(),\n                    max_wait_ms = max_wait.as_millis(),\n                    \"session pin wait timeout\"\n                );\n                return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"session pin wait timeout\".to_string()));\n            }\n\n            // Exponential backoff with cap\n            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(poll_delay)).await;\n            poll_delay = std::cmp::min(poll_delay * 2, 500); // Cap at 500ms\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the pinned group for a session (if any).\n    ///\n    /// Returns None if:\n    /// - Session doesn't exist\n    /// - Session has no pending write\n    /// - Session is expired\n    pub async fn get_pinned_group(&self, session_id: &str) -> Option<u32> {\n        if !self.config.enabled {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n        let session = sessions.get(session_id)?;\n\n        if session.is_expired() {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        if !session.has_pending_write() {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        session.pinned_group\n    }\n\n    /// Clear the pending write state for a session.\n    ///\n    /// Called when the write task completes.\n    pub async fn clear_pending_write(&self, session_id: &str) {\n        let mut pending = self.pending_writes.write().await;\n        pending.remove(session_id);\n\n        // Also clear the last_write_mtask_id in the session\n        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n        if let Some(session) = sessions.get_mut(session_id) {\n            session.last_write_mtask_id = None;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get session state.\n    pub async fn get_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> Option<SessionState> {\n        let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n        sessions.get(session_id).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Delete a session.\n    pub async fn delete_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> bool {\n        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n        let mut pending = self.pending_writes.write().await;\n        pending.remove(session_id);\n        sessions.remove(session_id).is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up expired sessions.\n    pub async fn prune_expired(&self) -> usize {\n        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n        let mut pending = self.pending_writes.write().await;\n\n        let now = millis_now();\n        let mut to_remove = Vec::new();\n\n        for (id, session) in sessions.iter() {\n            if session.is_expired() {\n                to_remove.push(id.clone());\n            }\n        }\n\n        for id in &to_remove {\n            sessions.remove(id);\n            pending.remove(id);\n        }\n\n        to_remove.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Get current session count.\n    pub async fn session_count(&self) -> usize {\n        let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n        sessions.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Handle pinned group failure - clear the pin for a session.\n    ///\n    /// Called when the pinned group for a session becomes unavailable.\n    /// Subsequent reads will use normal routing.\n    pub async fn handle_pinned_group_failure(&self, session_id: &str, failed_group: u32) -> bool {\n        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n        if let Some(session) = sessions.get_mut(session_id) {\n            if session.pinned_group == Some(failed_group) {\n                info!(\n                    session_id = %session_id,\n                    failed_group,\n                    \"clearing session pin due to group failure\"\n                );\n                session.pinned_group = None;\n                // Also clear pending write state since we can't guarantee visibility\n                session.last_write_mtask_id = None;\n                return true;\n            }\n        }\n        false\n    }\n\n    /// Get the wait strategy.\n    pub fn wait_strategy(&self) -> WaitStrategy {\n        match self.config.wait_strategy.as_str() {\n            \"block\" => WaitStrategy::Block,\n            \"route_pin\" => WaitStrategy::RoutePin,\n            _ => WaitStrategy::Block,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get max wait duration.\n    pub fn max_wait_duration(&self) -> Duration {\n        Duration::from_millis(self.config.max_wait_ms)\n    }\n\n    /// Update the session active count metric.\n    pub fn update_metrics(&self, active_count_fn: impl FnOnce(usize)) {\n        let count = self.sessions.blocking_read().len();\n        active_count_fn(count);\n    }\n\n    /// Check if session pinning is enabled.\n    pub fn is_enabled(&self) -> bool {\n        self.config.enabled\n    }\n}\n\n/// Wait strategy for reads with pending writes.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum WaitStrategy {\n    /// Block the read until the write completes.\n    Block,\n    /// Route to pinned group but don't wait for write.\n    RoutePin,\n}\n\nimpl Default for SessionManager {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(SessionPinningConfig::default())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\nfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as u64\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_config_default() {\n        let config = SessionPinningConfig::default();\n        assert!(config.enabled);\n        assert_eq!(config.ttl_seconds, 900);\n        assert_eq!(config.max_sessions, 100_000);\n        assert_eq!(config.wait_strategy, \"block\");\n        assert_eq!(config.max_wait_ms, 5000);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_record_write() {\n        let manager = SessionManager::default();\n        manager\n            .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-1\", \"mtask-123\".into(), 0)\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let session = manager.get_session(\"session-1\").await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(session.last_write_mtask_id, Some(\"mtask-123\".into()));\n        assert_eq!(session.pinned_group, Some(0));\n        assert!(session.has_pending_write());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_pinned_group() {\n        let manager = SessionManager::default();\n        manager\n            .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-1\", \"mtask-123\".into(), 2)\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let pinned = manager.get_pinned_group(\"session-1\").await;\n        assert_eq!(pinned, Some(2));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_clear_pending_write() {\n        let manager = manager.clear_pending_write(\"session-1\").await;\n\n        let session = manager.get_session(\"session-1\").await.unwrap();\n        assert!(!session.has_pending_write());\n\n        let pinned = manager.get_pinned_group(\"session-1\").await;\n        assert_eq!(pinned, None); // No pending write = no pin\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_max_sessions() {\n        let config = .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-1\", \"mtask-1\".into(), 0)\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n        manager\n            .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-2\", \"mtask-2\".into(), 0)\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n        manager\n            .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-3\", \"mtask-3\".into(), 0)\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // session-1 should be evicted (FIFO)\n        assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-1\").await.is_none());\n        assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-2\").await.is_some());\n assert!(!manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().has_pending_write());\n95\t\n96\t    // Read should not return pinned group (no pending write)\n97\t    let pinned_group = manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await;\n98\t    assert_eq!(pinned_group, None);\n99\t}\n100\t\n101\t#[tokio::test]\n102\tasync fn test_session_expiration() {\n103\t    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n104\t        ttl_seconds: 1, // 1 second TTL for testing\n105\t        ..Default::default()\n106\t    };\n107\t    let manager = test_manager(config);\n108\t\n109\t    let session_id = \"test-session-expire\";\n110\t    let mtask_id = \"mtask-expire\".to_string();\n111\t\n112\t    // Record a write\n113\t    manager\n114\t        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id, 0)\n115\t        .await\n116\t        .unwrap();\n117\t\n118\t    // Session should be active immediately\n119\t    assert!(!manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().is_expired());\n120\t\n121\t    // Wait for expiration\n122\t    sleep(Duration::from_millis(1100)).await;\n123\t\n124\t    // Session should be expired\n125\t    assert!(manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().is_expired());\n126\t\n127\t    // Pinned group should be None for expired session\n128\t    let pinned_group = SessionPinningConfig {\n135\t        max_sessions: 2,\n136\t        ..Default::default()\n137\t    };\n138\t    let manager = test_manager(config);\n139\t\n140\t    // Add first session\n141\t    manager\n142\t        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-1\", \"mtask-1\".to_string(), 0)\n143\t        .await\n144\t        .unwrap();\n145\t\n146\t    // Add second session\n147\t    manager\n148\t        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-2\", \"mtask-2\".to_string(), 0)\n149\t        .await\n150\t        .unwrap();\n151\t\n152\t    // Add third session (should evict first session)\n153\t    manager\n154\t        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-3\", \"mtask-3\".to_string(), 0)\n155\t        .await\n156\t        .unwrap();\n157\t\n158\t    // First session should be evicted\n159\t    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-1\").await.is_none());\n160\t\n161\t    // Second and third should still exist\n162\t    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-2\").await.is_some());\n163\t    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-3\").await.is_some());\n164\t}\n165\t\n166\t#[tokio::test]\n167\tasync fn test_pinned_group_failure_clears_pin() {\n168\t    let manager = default_manager();\n169\t\n170\t    let session_id = \"test-session-fail\";\n171\t    let mtask_id = \"mtask-fail\".to_string();\n172\t    let pinned_group = 1;\n173\t\n174\t    // Record a write\n175\t    manager\n176\t        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id, pinned_group)\n177\t        .await\n178\t        .unwrap();\n179\t\n180\t    // Verify pin is set\n181\t    assert_eq!(\n182\t        manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await,\n183\t        Some(pinned_group)\n184\t    );\n185\t\n186\t    // Simulate pinned group failure\n187\t    let cleared = manager\n188\t        .handle_pinned_group_failure(session_id, pinned_group)\n189\t        .await;\n190\t\n191\t    assert!(cleared);\n192\t\n193\t    // Pin should be cleared\n194\t    assert_eq!(manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await, None);\n195\t}\n196\t\n197\t#[tokio::test]\n198\tasync fn test_wait_strategy() {\n199\t    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n200\t        wait_strategy: \"route_pin\".to_string(),\n201\t        ..Default::default()\n202\t    };\n203\t    let manager = test_manager(config);\n204\t\n205\t    assert_eq!(manager.wait_strategy(), WaitStrategy::RoutePin);\n206\t\n207\t    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n208\t        wait_strategy: \"block\".to_string(),\n209\t        ..Default::default()\n210\t    };\n211\t    let manager = test_manager(config);\n212\t\n213\t    assert_eq!(manager.wait_strategy(), WaitStrategy::Block);\n214\t\n215\t    // Unknown strategy defaults to Block\n216\t    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n217\t        wait_strategy: \"unknown\".to_string(),\n218\t        ..Default::default()\n219\t    };\n220\t    let manager = test_manager(config);\n221\t\n222\t    assert_eq!(manager.wait_strategy(), WaitStrategy::Block);\n223\t}\n224\t\n225\t#[tokio::test]\n226\tasync fn test_prune_expired_sessions() {\n227\t    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n228\t        ttl_seconds: 1,\n229\t        ..Default::default()\n230\t    };\n231\t    let manager = test_manager(config);\n232\t\n233\t    // Add sessions\n234\t    manager\n235\t        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-active\", \"mtask-a\".to_string(), 0)\n236\t        .await\n237\t        .unwrap();\n238\t    manager\n239\t        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-expire\", \"mtask-b\".to_string(), 0)\n240\t        .await\n241\t        .unwrap();\n242\t\n243\t    // Wait for expiration\n244\t    sleep(Duration::from_millis(1100)).await;\n245\t\n246\t    // Refresh session-active to keep it alive\n247\t    manager\n248\t        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-active\", \"mtask-a-refreshed\".to_string(), 0)\n249\t        .await\n250\t        .unwrap();\n251\t\n252\t    // Add another active session\n253\t    manager\n254\t        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-active-2\", \"mtask-c\".to_string(), 0)\n255\t        .await\n256\t        .unwrap();\n257\t\n258\t    // Prune expired\n259\t    let pruned = manager.prune_expired().await;\n260\t\n261\t    // Should have pruned 1 expired session (session-expire)\n262\t    assert_eq!(pruned, 1);\n263\t\n264\t    // Expired session should be gone\n265\t    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-expire\").await.is_none());\n266\t\n267\t    // Active sessions should still exist\n268\t    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-active\").await.is_some());\n269\t    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-active-2\").await.is_some());\n270\t}\n271\t\n272\t#[tokio::test]\n273\tasync fn test_delete_session() {\n274\t    let manager = default_manager();\n275\t\n276\t    let session_id = \"test-session-delete\";\n277\t    manager\n278\t        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, \"mtask-delete\".to_string(), 0)\n279\t        .await\n280\t        .unwrap();\n281\t\n282\t    // Verify session exists\n283\t    assert!(manager.get_session(session_id).await.is_some());\n284\t\n285\t    // Delete session\n286\t    let deleted = Delete again should return false\n293\t    let deleted_again = manager.delete_session(session_id).await;\n294\t    assert!(!deleted_again);\n295\t}\n296\t\n297\t#[tokio::test]\n298\tasync fn test_session_count() {\n299\t    let manager = default_manager();\n300\t\n301\t    assert_eq!(manager.session_count().await, 0);\n302\t\n303\t    manager\n304\t        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-1\", \"mtask-1\".to_string(), 0)\n305\t        .await\n306\t        .unwrap();\n307\t\n308\t    assert_eq!(manager.session_count().await, 1);\n309\t\n310\t    manager\n311\t        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-2\", \"mtask-2\".to_string(), 0)\n312\t        .await\n313\t        .unwrap();\n314\t\n315\t    assert_eq!(manager.session_count().await, 2);\n316\t\n317\t    manager.delete_session(\"session-1\").await;\n318\t\n319\t    assert_eq!(manager.session_count().await, 1);\n320\t}\n321\t\n322\t#[tokio::test]\n323\tasync fn test_multiple_writes_same_session_preserves_pin() {\n324\t    let manager = default_manager();\n325\t\n326\t    let session_id = \"test-session-multi\";\n327\t    let first_group = 2;\n328\t\n329\t    // First write pins to group 2\n330\t    manager\n331\t        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, \"mtask-1\".to_string(), first_group)\n332\t        .await\n333\t        .unwrap();\n334\t\n335\t    assert_eq!(\n336\t        manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().pinned_group,\n337\t        Some(first_group)\n338\t    );\n339\t\n340\t    // Second write tries to pin to group 3 (should be ignored)\n341\t    manager\n342\t        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, \"mtask-2\".to_string(), 3)\n343\t        .await\n344\t        .unwrap();\n345\t\n346\t    // Pin should still be group 2 (first write wins)\n347\t    assert_eq!(\n348\t        manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().pinned_group,\n349\t        Some(first_group)\n350\t    );\n351\t}\n352\t\n353\t#[tokio::test]\n354\tasync fn test_max_wait_duration() {\n355\t    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n356\t        max_wait_ms: 10000,\n357\t        ..Default::default()\n358\t    };\n359\t    let manager = test_manager(config);\n360\t\n361\t    assert_eq!(manager.max_wait_duration().as_millis(), 10000);\n362\t}\n363\t\n364\t#[tokio::test]\n365\tasync fn test_enabled_flag() {\n366\t    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n367\t        enabled: false,\n368\t        ..Default::default()\n369\t    };\n370\t    let manager = test_manager(config);\n371\t\n372\t    assert!(!manager.is_enabled());\n373\t\n374\t    // When disabled, record_write should succeed but be no-op\n375\t    manager\n376\t        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-1\", \"mtask-1\".to_string(), 0)\n377\t        .await\n378\t        .unwrap();\n379\t\n380\t    // Session should not be recorded\n381\t    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-1\").await.is_none());\n382\t}\n383\t\n384\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n385\t// Integration tests: read-your-writes with task registry (plan §13.6)\n386\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n387\t\n388\tuse miroir_core::task::{MiroirTask, TaskRegistry, TaskStatus, NodeTask, NodeTaskStatus, TaskFilter};\n389\tuse miroir_core::error::Result;\n390\tuse miroir_core::error::MiroirError;\n391\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n392\t\n393\t/// Mock task registry for testing session pinning wait behavior.\n394\tstruct MockTaskRegistry {\n395\t    tasks: Arc<tokio::sync::RwLock<HashMap<String, MiroirTask>>>,\n396\t}\n397\t\n398\timpl MockTaskRegistry {\n399\t    fn new() -> Self {\n400\t        Self {\n401\t            tasks: Arc::new(tokio::sync::RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n402\t        }\n403\t    }\n404\t\n405\t    /// Add a task with a specific status.\n406\t    async fn add_task(&self, mtask_id: String, status: TaskStatus) {\n407\t        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n408\t        tasks.insert(\n409\t            mtask_id.clone(),\n410\t            MiroirTask {\n411\t                miroir_id: mtask_id.clone(),\n412\t                node_tasks: HashMap::new(),\n413\t                node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n414\t                status,\n415\t                created_at: std::time::SystemTime::now()\n416\t                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n417\t                    .unwrap()\n418\t                    .as_millis() as u64,\n419\t                started_at: None,\n420\t                finished_at: None,\n421\t                index_uid: None,\n422\t                task_type: None,\n423\t                error: None,\n424\t            },\n425\t        );\n426\t    }\n427\t\n428\t    /// Update a task's status.\n429\t    async fn update_task(&self, mtask_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) {\n430\t        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n431\t        if let Some(task) = tasks.get_mut(mtask_id) {\n432\t            task.status = status;\n433\t        }\n434\t    }\n435\t}\n436\t\n437\timpl TaskRegistry for MockTaskRegistry {\n438\t    fn get(&self, mtask_id: &str) -> Result<Option<MiroirTask>> {\n439\t        let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n440\t            .map_err(|_| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"no tokio runtime\".to_string()))?;\n441\t        rt.block_on(async {\n442\t            let tasks = self.tasks.read().await;\n443\t            Ok(tasks.get(mtask_id).cloned())\n444\t        })\n445\t    }\n446\t\n447\t    fn register_with_metadata(\n448\t        &self,\n449\t        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n450\t        _index_uid: Option<String>,\n451\t        _task_type: Option<String>,\n452\t    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n453\t        let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n454\t            .map_err(|_| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"no tokio runtime\".to_string()))?;\n455\t        rt.block_on(async {\n456\t            let mtask_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n457\t            // Convert u64 node task IDs to NodeTask structures\n458\t            let node_tasks_mapped = node_tasks.into_iter()\n459\t                .map(|(node_id, task_uid)| {\n460\t                    (node_id, NodeTask {\n461\t                        task_uid,\n462\t                        status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n463\t                    })\n464\t                })\n465\t                .collect();\n466\t\n467\t            let task = MiroirTask {\n468\t                miroir_id: mtask_id.clone(),\n469\t                node_tasks: node_tasks_mapped,\n470\t                node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n471\t                status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n472\t                created_at: std::time::SystemTime::now()\n473\t                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n474\t                    .unwrap()\n475\t                    .as_millis() as u64,\n476\t                started_at: None,\n477\t                finished_at: None,\n478\t                index_uid: None,\n479\t                task_type: None,\n480\t                error: None,\n481\t            };\n482\t            let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n483\t            tasks.insert(mtask_id.clone(), task.clone());\n484\t            Ok(task)\n485\t        })\n486\t    }\n487\t\n488\t    fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n489\t        let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n490\t            .map_err(|_| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"no tokio runtime\".to_string()))?;\n491\t        rt.block_on(async {\n492\t            let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n493\t            if let Some(task) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n494\t                task.status = status;\n495\t            }\n496\t            Ok(())\n497\t        })\n498\t    }\n499\t\n500\t    fn update_node_task(\n501\t        &self,\n502\t        _miroir_id: &str,\n503\t        _node_id: &str,\n504\t        _node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n505\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n506\t        Ok(())\n507\t    }\n508\t\n509\t    fn list(&self, _filter: TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n510\t        Ok(Vec::new())\n511\t    }\n512\t\n513\t    fn count(&self) -> usize {\n514\t        0\n515\t    }\n516\t}\n517\t\n518\t/// Acceptance test 1: Write + session + immediate read with `block` → read sees the write.\n519\t///\n520\t/// This test verifies that when using the \"block\" wait strategy, a read with a session\n521\t/// header will wait for the pending write to complete before proceeding.\n522\t///\n523\t/// Note: This is a simplified test that verifies the session pin is created correctly.\n524\t/// The actual blocking behavior is tested in integration tests with a real task registry.\n525\t#[tokio::test]\n526\tasync fn acceptance_write_session_read_with_block_strategy() {\n527\t    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n528\t        wait_strategy: \"block\".to_string(),\n529\t        max_wait_ms: 5000,\n530\t        ..Default::default()\n531\t    };\n532\t    let manager = test_manager(config);\n533\t\n534\t    let session_id = \"test-session-block-read\";\n535\t    let mtask_id = \"mtask-block-123\".to_string();\n536\t\n537\t    // Record a write with session pinning\n538\t    manager\n539\t        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id, 0)\n540\t        .await\n541\t        .unwrap();\n542\t\n543\t    // Verify the session is pinned\n544\t    assert_eq!(\n545\t        manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await,\n546\t        Some(0)\n547\t    );\n548\t\n549\t    // Verify the wait strategy is block\n550\t    assert_eq!(manager.wait_strategy(), WaitStrategy::Block);\n551\t\n552\t    // After clearing the pending write, no pin should be returned\n553\t    manager.clear_pending_write(session_id).await;\n554\t    let pinned_group = manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await;\n555\t    assert_eq!(pinned_group, None);\n556\t}\n557\t\n558\t/// Acceptance test 2: Write + session + immediate read with `route_pin` → read routed to pinned group.\n559\t///\n560\t/// This test verifies that when using the \"route_pin\" wait strategy, a read with a session\n561\t/// header routes to the pinned group without waiting for the write to complete.\n562\t#[tokio::test]\n563\tasync fn acceptance_write_session_read_with_route_pin_strategy() {\n564\t    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n565\t        wait_strategy: \"route_pin\".to_string(),\n566\t        max_wait_ms: 5000,\n567\t        ..Default::default()\n568\t    };\n569\t    let manager = test_manager(config);\n570\t    let task_registry = Arc::new(MockTaskRegistry::new());\n571\t\n572\t    let session_id = \"test-session-route-pin\";\n573\t    let mtask_id = \"mtask-route-pin-123\".to_string();\n574\t\n575\t    // Record a write with session pinning\n576\t    manager\n577\t        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id, 1)\n578\t        .await\n579\t        .unwrap();\n580\t\n581\t    // Verify the session is pinned to group 1\n582\t    assert_eq!(\n583\t        manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await,\n584\t        Some(1)\n585\t    );\n586\t\n587\t    // With route_pin strategy, we don't wait - just check the pinned group\n588\t    let pinned_group = manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await;\n589\t    assert_eq!(pinned_group, Some(1));\n590\t\n591\t    // The pending write should still be present (not cleared by just reading)\n592\t    assert!(manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().has_pending_write());\n593\t}\n594\t\n595\t/// Acceptance test 3: Pinned group fails mid-session → pin cleared; read succeeds via another group.\n596\t///\n597\t/// This test verifies that when the pinned group fails, the session pin is cleared\n598\t/// and subsequent reads can succeed via other groups.\n599\t#[tokio::test]\n600\tasync fn acceptance_pinned_group_failure_clears_pin() {\n601\t    let manager = default_manager();\n602\t\n603\t    let session_id = \"test-session-group-fail\";\n604\t    let mtask_id = \"mtask-group-fail\".to_string();\n605\t    let pinned_group = 2;\n606\t\n607\t    // Record a write that pins to group 2\n608\t    manager\n609\t        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id, pinned_group)\n610\t        .await\n611\t        .unwrap();\n612\t\n613\t    // Verify the session is pinned\n614\t    assert_eq!(\n615\t        manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await,\n616\t        Some(pinned_group)\n617\t    );\n618\t\n619\t    // Simulate group 2 failing\n620\t    let cleared = manager\n621\t        .handle_pinned_group_failure(session_id, pinned_group)\n622\t        .await;\n623\t\n624\t    assert!(cleared, \"Pin should be cleared when pinned group fails\");\n625\t\n626\t    // Pin should be cleared\n627\t    assert_eq!(\n628\t        manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await,\n629\t        None,\n630\t        \"After group failure, no pinned group should be returned\"\n631\t    );\n632\t\n633\t    // The session should still exist, but with no pin\n634\t    let session = manager.get_session(session_id).await;\n635\t    assert!(session.is_some(), \"Session should still exist\");\n636\t    assert_eq!(session.unwrap().pinned_group, None, \"Session should have no pinned group\");\n637\t}\n638\t\n639\t/// Acceptance test 4: Session TTL expiry and LRU eviction.\n640\t///\n641\t/// This test verifies that:\n642\t/// - Sessions expire after their TTL\n643\t/// - LRU eviction happens when max_sessions is reached\n644\t#[tokio::test]\n645\tasync fn acceptance_session_ttl_and_lru_eviction() {\n646\t    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n647\t        ttl_seconds: 1,\n648\t        max_sessions: 3,\n649\t        ..Default::default()\n650\t    };\n651\t    let manager = test_manager(config);\n652\t\n653\t    // Create sessions up to the limit\n654\t    manager\n655\t        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-1\", \"mtask-1\".to_string(), 0)\n656\t        .await\n657\t        .unwrap();\n658\t    manager\n659\t        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-2\", \"mtask-2\".to_string(), 0)\n660\t        .await\n661\t        .unwrap();\n662\t    manager\n663\t        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-3\", \"mtask-3\".to_string(), 0)\n664\t        .await\n665\t        .unwrap();\n666\t\n667\t    // All three should exist\n668\t    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-1\").await.is_some());\n669\t    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-2\").await.is_some());\n670\t    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-3\").await.is_some());\n671\t\n672\t    // Adding a 4th session should evict the oldest (session-1)\n673\t    manager\n674\t        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-4\", \"mtask-4\".to_string(), 0)\n675\t        .await\n676\t        .unwrap();\n677\t\n678\t    // Session-1 should be evicted\n679\t    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-1\").await.is_none());\n680\t\n681\t    // Sessions 2, 3, 4 should still exist\n682\t    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-2\").await.is_some());\n683\t    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-3\").await.is_some());\n684\t    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-4\").await.is_some());\n685\t\n686\t    // Wait for TTL expiration\n687\t    sleep(Duration::from_millis(1100)).await;\n688\t\n689\t    // All remaining sessions should be expired\n690\t    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-2\").await.unwrap().is_expired());\n691\t Session pinning acceptance tests (plan §13.6).\n//!\n//! Tests read-your-writes consistency via session pinning:\n//! - Write with session header → session pinned to first-quorum group\n//! - Read with session header → routes to pinned group if pending write\n//! - Block strategy: read blocks until write completes\n//! - RoutePin strategy: read routes to pinned group without waiting\n//! - Session TTL and LRU eviction\n//! - Pinned group failure handling\n\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::time::{sleep, Duration};\nuse miroir_core::session_pinning::{SessionManager, SessionPinningConfig, WaitStrategy};\n\n/// Helper to create a test session manager with custom config.\nfn test_manager(config: SessionPinningConfig) -> SessionManager {\n    SessionManager::new(config)\n}\n\n/// Helper to create a default test session manager.\nfn default_manager() -> SessionManager {\n    test_manager(SessionPinningConfig::default())\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_write_records_session_pin() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    // Record a write with session header\n    let session_id = \"test-session-1\";\n    let mtask_id = \"mtask-123\".to_string();\n    let first_quorum_group = 2;\n\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id.clone(), first_quorum_group)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify session is recorded\n    let session = manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(session.last_write_mtask_id, Some(mtask_id));\n    assert_eq!(session.pinned_group, Some(first_quorum_group));\n    assert!(session.has_pending_write());\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_read_with_pending_write_returns_pinned_group() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    // Record a write\n    let session_id = \"test-session-2\";\n    let mtask_id = \"mtask-456\".to_string();\n    let first_quorum_group = 1;\n\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id, first_quorum_group)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Get pinned group for read\n    let pinned_group = manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await;\n    assert_eq!(pinned_group, Some(first_quorum_group));\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_read_without_session_returns_none() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    // No session header\n    let pinned_group = manager.get_pinned_group(\"nonexistent\").await;\n    assert_eq!(pinned_group, None);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_write_without_clearing_pending_write() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    let session_id = \"test-session-3\";\n    let mtask_id = \"mtask-789\".to_string();\n\n    // Record a write\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id.clone(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify pending write exists\n    assert!(manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().has_pending_write());\n\n    // Clear pending write (simulating task completion)\n    manager.clear_pending_write(session_id).await;\n\n    // Verify no pending write\n    assert!(!manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().has_pending_write());\n\n    // Read should not return pinned group (no pending write)\n    let pinned_group = manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await;\n    assert_eq!(pinned_group, None);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_session_expiration() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        ttl_seconds: 1, // 1 second TTL for testing\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n    let session_id = \"test-session-expire\";\n    let mtask_id = \"mtask-expire\".to_string();\n\n    // Record a write\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id, 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Session should be active immediately\n    assert!(!manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().is_expired());\n\n    // Wait for expiration\n    sleep(Duration::from_millis(1100)).await;\n\n    // Session should be expired\n    assert!(manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().is_expired());\n\n    // Pinned group should be None for expired session\n    let pinned_group = manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await;\n    assert_eq!(pinned_group, None);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_max_sessions_lru_eviction() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        max_sessions: 2,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n    // Add first session\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-1\", \"mtask-1\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Add second session\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-2\", \"mtask-2\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Add third session (should evict first session)\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-3\", \"mtask-3\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // First session should be evicted\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-1\").await.is_none());\n\n    // Second and third should still exist\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-2\").await.is_some());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-3\").await.is_some());\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_pinned_group_failure_clears_pin() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    let session_id = \"test-session-fail\";\n    let mtask_id = \"mtask-fail\".to_string();\n    let pinned_group = 1;\n\n    // Record a write\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id, pinned_group)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify pin is set\n    assert_eq!(\n pinned_group)\n        .await;\n\n    assert!(cleared);\n\n    // Pin should be cleared\n    assert_eq!(manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await, None);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_wait_strategy() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        wait_strategy: \"route_pin\".to_string(),\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n WaitStrategy::Block);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_prune_expired_sessions() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        ttl_seconds: 1,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n    // Add sessions\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-active\", \"mtask-a\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-expire\", \"mtask-b\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Wait for expiration\n    sleep(Duration::from_millis(1100)).await;\n\n    // Refresh session-active to keep it alive\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-active\", \"mtask-a-refreshed\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Add another active session\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-active-2\", \"mtask-c\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Prune expired\n    let pruned = manager.prune_expired().await;\n\n    // Should have pruned 1 expired session (session-expire)\n    assert_eq!(pruned, 1);\n\n    // Expired session should be gone\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-expire\").await.is_none());\n\n    // Active sessions should still exist\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-active\").await.is_some());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-active-2\").await.is_some());\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_delete_session() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    let session_id = \"test-session-delete\";\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, \"mtask-delete\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n manager.delete_session(session_id).await;\n    assert!(!deleted_again);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_session_count() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n test_multiple_writes_same_session_preserves_pin() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    let session_id = \"test-session-multi\";\n    let first_group = 2;\n\n    // First write pins to group 2\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, \"mtask-1\".to_string(), first_group)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(\n        manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().pinned_group,\n        Some(first_group)\n    );\n\n    // Second write tries to pin to group 3 (should be ignored)\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, \"mtask-2\".to_string(), 3)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Pin should still be group 2 (first write wins)\n    assert_eq!(\n        manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().pinned_group,\n        Some(first_group)\n    );\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_max_wait_duration() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        max_wait_ms: 10000,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n    assert_eq!(manager.max_wait_duration().as_millis(), 10000);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_enabled_flag() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        enabled: false,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Integration tests: read-your-writes with task registry (plan §13.6)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nuse miroir_core::task::{MiroirTask, TaskRegistry, TaskStatus, NodeTask, NodeTaskStatus, TaskFilter};\nuse miroir_core::error::Result;\nuse miroir_core::error::MiroirError;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n/// Mock task registry for testing session pinning wait behavior.\nstruct MockTaskRegistry {\n    tasks: Arc<tokio::sync::RwLock<HashMap<String, MiroirTask>>>,\n}\n\nimpl MockTaskRegistry {\n    fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            tasks: Arc::new(tokio::sync::RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a task with a specific status.\n    async fn add_task(&self, mtask_id: String, status: TaskStatus) {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        tasks.insert(\n            mtask_id.clone(),\n            MiroirTask {\n                miroir_id: mtask_id.clone(),\n                node_tasks: HashMap::new(),\n                node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                status,\n                created_at: std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                    .unwrap()\n                    .as_millis() as u64,\n                started_at: None,\n                finished_at: None,\n                index_uid: None,\n                task_type: None,\n                error: None,\n            },\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Update a task's status.\n    async fn update_task(&self, mtask_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        if let Some(task) = tasks.get_mut(mtask_id) {\n            task.status = status;\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl TaskRegistry for MockTaskRegistry {\n    fn get(&self, mtask_id: &str) -> Result<Option<MiroirTask>> {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n            .map_err(|_| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"no tokio runtime\".to_string()))?;\n        rt.block_on(async {\n            let tasks = self.tasks.read().await;\n            Ok(tasks.get(mtask_id).cloned())\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn register_with_metadata(\n        &self,\n        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        _index_uid: Option<String>,\n        _task_type: Option<String>,\n    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n            .map_err(|_| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"no tokio runtime\".to_string()))?;\n        rt.block_on(async {\n            let mtask_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n            // Convert u64 node task IDs to NodeTask structures\n            let node_tasks_mapped = node_tasks.into_iter()\n                .map(|(node_id, task_uid)| {\n                    (node_id, NodeTask {\n                        task_uid,\n                        status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n                    })\n                })\n                .collect();\n\n            let task = MiroirTask {\n                miroir_id: mtask_id.clone(),\n                node_tasks: node_tasks_mapped,\n                node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n                created_at: std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                    .unwrap()\n                    .as_millis() as u64,\n                started_at: None,\n                finished_at: None,\n                index_uid: None,\n                task_type: None,\n                error: None,\n            };\n            let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n            tasks.insert(mtask_id.clone(), task.clone());\n            Ok(task)\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n            .map_err(|_| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"no tokio runtime\".to_string()))?;\n        rt.block_on(async {\n            let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n            if let Some(task) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n                task.status = status;\n            }\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn update_node_task(\n        &self,\n        _miroir_id: &str,\n        _node_id: &str,\n        _node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn list(&self, _filter: TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n        Ok(Vec::new())\n    }\n\n    fn count(&self) -> usize {\n        0\n    }\n}\n\n/// Acceptance test 1: Write + session + immediate read with `block` → read sees the write.\n///\n/// This test verifies that when using the \"block\" wait strategy, a read with a session\n/// header will wait for the pending write to complete before proceeding.\n///\n/// Note: This is a simplified test that verifies the session pin is created correctly.\n/// The actual blocking behavior is tested in integration tests with a real task registry.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn acceptance_write_session_read_with_block_strategy() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        wait_strategy: \"block\".to_string(),\n        max_wait_ms: 5000,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n    let session_id = \"test-session-block-read\";\n    let mtask_id = \"mtask-block-123\".to_string();\n\n    // Record a write with session pinning\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id, 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify the session is pinned\n    assert_eq!(\n        manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await,\n        Some(0)\n    );\n\n    // Verify the wait strategy is block\n    assert_eq!(manager.wait_strategy(), WaitStrategy::Block);\n\n    // After clearing the pending write, no pin should be returned\n    manager.clear_pending_write(session_id).await;\n    let pinned_group = manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await;\n    assert_eq!(pinned_group, None);\n}\n\n/// Acceptance test 2: Write + session + immediate read with `route_pin` → read routed to pinned group.\n///\n/// This test verifies that when using the \"route_pin\" wait strategy, a read with a session\n/// header routes to the pinned group without waiting for the write to complete.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn acceptance_write_session_read_with_route_pin_strategy() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        wait_strategy: \"route_pin\".to_string(),\n        max_wait_ms: 5000,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n    let task_registry = Arc::new(MockTaskRegistry::new());\n\n    let session_id = \"test-session-route-pin\";\n    let mtask_id = \"mtask-route-pin-123\".to_string();\n\n    // Record a write with session pinning\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id, 1)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify the session is pinned to group 1\n    assert_eq!(\n        manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await,\n        Some(1)\n    );\n\n    // With route_pin strategy, we don't wait - just check the pinned group\n    let pinned_group = manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await;\n    assert_eq!(pinned_group, Some(1));\n\n    // The pending write should still be present (not cleared by just reading)\n    assert!(manager.get_session(session_id).await.unwrap().has_pending_write());\n}\n\n/// Acceptance test 3: Pinned group fails mid-session → pin cleared; read succeeds via another group.\n///\n/// This test verifies that when the pinned group fails, the session pin is cleared\n/// and subsequent reads can succeed via other groups.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn acceptance_pinned_group_failure_clears_pin() {\n    let manager = default_manager();\n\n    let session_id = \"test-session-group-fail\";\n    let mtask_id = \"mtask-group-fail\".to_string();\n    let pinned_group = 2;\n\n    // Record a write that pins to group 2\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(session_id, mtask_id, pinned_group)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify the session is pinned\n    assert_eq!(\n        manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await,\n        Some(pinned_group)\n    );\n\n    // Simulate group 2 failing\n    let cleared = manager\n        .handle_pinned_group_failure(session_id, pinned_group)\n        .await;\n\n    assert!(cleared, \"Pin should be cleared when pinned group fails\");\n\n    // Pin should be cleared\n    assert_eq!(\n        manager.get_pinned_group(session_id).await,\n        None,\n        \"After group failure, no pinned group should be returned\"\n    );\n\n    // The session should still exist, but with no pin\n pinned group\");\n}\n\n/// Acceptance test 4: Session TTL expiry and LRU eviction.\n///\n/// This test verifies that:\n/// - Sessions expire after their TTL\n/// - LRU eviction happens when max_sessions is reached\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn acceptance_session_ttl_and_lru_eviction() {\n    let config = SessionPinningConfig {\n        ttl_seconds: 1,\n        max_sessions: 3,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let manager = test_manager(config);\n\n    // Create sessions up to the limit\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-1\", \"mtask-1\".to_string(), .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-3\", \"mtask-3\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // All three should exist\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-1\").await.is_some());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-2\").await.is_some());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-3\").await.is_some());\n\n    // Adding a 4th session should evict the oldest (session-1)\n    manager\n        .record_write_with_quorum(\"session-4\", \"mtask-4\".to_string(), 0)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Session-1 should be evicted\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-1\").await.is_none());\n\n    // Sessions 2, 3, 4 should still exist\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-2\").await.is_some());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-3\").await.is_some());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-4\").await.is_some());\n\n    // Wait for TTL expiration\n    sleep(Duration::from_millis(1100)).await;\n\n    // All remaining sessions should be expired\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-2\").await.unwrap().is_expired());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-3\").await.unwrap().is_expired());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-4\").await.unwrap().is_expired());\n\n    // Prune should remove all expired sessions\n    let pruned = manager.prune_expired().await;\n    assert_eq!(pruned, 3);\n\n    // No sessions should remain\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-2\").await.is_none());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-3\").await.is_none());\n    assert!(manager.get_session(\"session-4\").await.is_none());\n}\n\n/// Integration test: Session pinning with scatter plan for pinned group.\n///\n/// This test verifies that the `dfs_query_then_fetch` pattern:\n127\t/// 1. **Preflight phase**: Send term-frequency query to all shards, aggregate\n128\t///    global document frequencies at the coordinator.\n129\t/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n130\t///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n131\t///\n132\t/// Returns };\n225\t\n226\t    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n227\t    let (pinned_group, strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n228\t        if let state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n246\t                            (None, Some(\"block_success\"))\n247\t                        }\n248\t                        Ok(false) => {\n249\t         }\n264\t                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n265\t                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n266\t sync context\n386\t                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n387\t                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n388\t                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n389\t                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n390\t                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n391\t                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n392\t                        })\n393\t                    })\n394\t                },\n395\t            );\n396\t\n397\t            match plan_result {\n398\t                Some(p) => p,\n399\t                None => {\n400\t                    // No covering set could be assembled after filtering by version floor\n401\t                    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n402\t                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n403\t                        format!(\n404\t                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n405\t                            floor, index\n406\t                        ),\n407\t                    );\n408\t                    return Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE);\n409\t                }\n410\t            }\n411\t        } else {\n412\t            // No version floor requested, use normal planning\n413\t            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n414\t        }\n415\t    };\n416\t    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n417\t\n418\t    // Record scatter fan-out size failed\");\n456\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n457\t    })?;\n458\t\n459\t    // Drop topology lock before building response\n460\t    drop(topo);\n461\t\n462\t    // Strip internal fields from hits\n463\t    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n464\t        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n465\t    }\n466\t\n467\t    // Build response body\n468\t    let mut body = .status(StatusCode::OK)\n482\t        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n483\t\n484\t    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent header if a broadcast is in flight (plan §13.5)\n485\t    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n486\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n487\t    }\n488\t\n489\t    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Version header if we have a version for this index\n490\t    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n491\t    if current_version > 0 {\n492\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n493\t    }\n494\t\n495\t    if result.degraded {\n496\t        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n497\t    }\n498\t\n499\t    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n500\t        let mut sorted_shards = search_multi_targets(\n536\t    targets: Vec<String>,\n537\t    body: SearchRequestBody,\n538\t    state: Arc<AppState>,\n539\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n540\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n541\t                warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n583\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n584\t                }\n585\t            }\n586\t        }\n587\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n588\t        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n589\t            &source_ip,\n590\t            limit,\n591\t        // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n606\t            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n607\t            match strategy {\n608\t
