let r = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(!r.degraded);\n        assert!(!r.hits.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    /// Integration test for dfs_query_then_fetch with severely skewed shard distribution.\n    ///\n    /// This test simulates the scenario described in miroir-yio:\n    /// - Shard 0: Normal (1,000 docs, term df = 100)\n    /// - Shard 1: 100x normal (100,000 docs, term df = 10,000)\n    /// - Shard 2: Near-empty (10 docs, term df = 1)\n    ///\n    /// Without global IDF preflight, each shard would compute different local IDF values:\n    /// - Shard 0: IDF ≈ log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    /// - Shard 1: IDF ≈ log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    /// - Shard 2: IDF ≈ log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.8\n    ///\n    /// With global IDF preflight, all shards use the same IDF:\n    /// - Global: N = 101,010, df = 10,101\n    /// - IDF ≈ log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    ///\n    /// This ensures scores are comparable across shards, enabling correct score-based merge.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_skewed_shards_global_idf_aggregation() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3);\n\n        // Simulate severely skewed shard distribution\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Shard 0: Normal distribution\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 1000,\n            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 100 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 80 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Shard 1: 100x normal (severely skewed)\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 100_000,\n            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 10_000 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 8_000 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Shard 2: Near-empty\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 10,\n            avg_doc_length: 45.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Execute preflight to get global IDF\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![\"machine\".into(), \"learning\".into()],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Verify global aggregation\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 101_010);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().df, 10_101);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().df, 8_081);\n\n        // Verify global IDF is the same for all shards\n        // Expected IDF for \"machine\": log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1\n        let expected_idf_machine: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 10101.0 + 0.5) / (10101.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let actual_idf_machine = global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().idf;\n        assert!((actual_idf_machine - expected_idf_machine).abs() < 0.001);\n\n        // Expected IDF for \"learning\": log((101010 - 8081 + 0.5) / (8081 + 0.5)) + 1\n        let expected_idf_learning: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 8081.0 + 0.5) / (8081.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let actual_idf_learning = global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().idf;\n        assert!((actual_idf_learning - expected_idf_learning).abs() < 0.001);\n\n        // Verify that without global IDF, local IDF values would differ significantly\n        // Shard 0 local IDF for \"machine\": log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.19\n        // Shard 1 local IDF for \"machine\": log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.20\n        // Shard 2 local IDF for \"machine\": log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.85\n        let local_idf_shard_0: f64 = ((1000.0_f64 - 100.0 + 0.5) / (100.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let local_idf_shard_2: f64 = ((10.0_f64 - 1.0 + 0.5) / (1.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - local_idf_shard_0).abs() > 0.2, \"Local IDF values should differ significantly\");\n        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - actual_idf_machine).abs() > 0.3, \"Global IDF should be closer to large-shard local IDF\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that DFS preflight handles empty query terms gracefully.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_empty_query_terms() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n        let c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 0);\n        assert!(global_idf.terms.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    /// Test that DFS preflight handles partial failures gracefully.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_partial_failure() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Node 0 returns valid data\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50000,\n            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]),\n        });\n\n        // Node 1 returns valid data\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000,\n            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n        });\n\n        // Node 2 fails\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![\"test\".into()],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        // Should aggregate from successful nodes only\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, injects showRankingScore: true and sets limit to offset + limit.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_injects_show_ranking_score() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: Some(\"rust programming\".into()),\n            offset: 10,\n            limit: 20,\n            filter: Some(serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")),\n            facets: Some(vec![\"category\".into(), \"tags\".into()]),\n            ranking_score: false, // Client didn't request scores\n            body: serde_json::json!({\"custom\": \"field\"}),\n            global_idf: None,\n        };\n\n        let body = req.to_node_body();\n\n        // showRankingScore must be true unconditionally\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"showRankingScore\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(true)));\n\n        // limit must be offset + limit (coordinator pagination)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(30)));\n\n        // offset must be 0 (coordinator handles offset)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n\n        // query must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"q\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"rust programming\")));\n\n        // filter must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"filter\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")));\n\n        // facets must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"facets\"), Some(&serde_json::json!([\"category\", \"tags\"])));\n\n        // custom body field must be preserved\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"custom\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"field\")));\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body works with minimal request.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_minimal_request() {\n        let req = SearchRequest }\n\n    /// Test group fallback when primary group has failed nodes.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_group_fallback_on_partial_failure() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        // Group 0: 2 nodes\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), \"http://g0-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        // Group 1: 2 nodes (healthy fallback targets)\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16); // query_seq=0 → group 0\n        assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 0);\n\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Set up responses: all shards on group 1 nodes return valid data\n        let response_1 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"doc1\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n        });\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), response_1.clone());\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), response_1);\n\n        // All nodes in group 0 fail\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let req = make_req();\n\n        // With fallback policy, shards should succeed via group 1\n        let result = execute_scatter(plan, &c, req, &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Should have succeeded via fallback (group 1)\n        assert!(!result.partial, \"Fallback should have succeeded\");\n        assert!(result.failed_shards.is_empty(), \"No shards should have failed after fallback\");\n        assert!(!result.shard_pages.is_empty(), \"Should have shard pages from fallback\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test group fallback when both groups are down.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_group_fallback_all_groups_down() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16);\n test_partial_policy_no_fallback() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16);\n        let mut c = \"type\")]\n53\t    error_type: Option<String>,\n54\t}\n55\t\n56\t/// Response for write operations.\n57\t#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\n58\tpub struct DocumentsWriteResponse {\n59\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n60\t    taskUid: Option<String>, // Changed to String to hold mtask-<uuid>\n61\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n62\t    indexUid: Option<String>,\n63\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n64\t    status: Option<String>,\n65\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n66\t    error: Option<String>,\n67\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n68\t    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n69\t    error_type: Option<String>,\n70\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n71\t    code: Option<String>,\n72\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n73\t    link: Option<String>,\n74\t}\n75\t\n76\t/// Header name for degraded write responses.\n77\tpub const HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED: &str = \"X-Miroir-Degraded\";\n78\t\n79\t/// Quorum tracking state for write operations.\n80\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n81\tstruct QuorumState {\n82\t    /// Per-group ACK counts: group_id -> successful_ack_count\n83\t    group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n84\t    /// Per-group total node counts: group_id -> total_nodes_attempted\n85\t    group_totals: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n86\t    /// Groups that met quorum: group_id -> true\n87\t    groups_met_quorum: HashMap<u32, bool>,\n88\t    /// Total degraded groups count\n89\t    degraded_groups: u32,\n90\t}\n91\t\n92\timpl QuorumState {\n93\t    /// Record a write attempt to a node.\n94\t    fn record_attempt(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n95\t        *self.group_totals.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n96\t    }\n97\t\n98\t    /// Record a successful write ACK from a node.\n99\t    fn record_success(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n100\t        *self.group_acks.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n101\t    }\n102\t\n103\t    /// Record a failed write attempt from a node.\n104\t    fn record_failure(&mut self, _group_id: u32) {\n105\t        // Track that this group had a failure\n106\t        // Degraded is determined after checking quorum\n107\t    }\n108\t\n109\t    /// Check if a group has met quorum: floor(RF/2) + 1 ACKs required.\n110\t    fn check_group_quorum(&mut self, group_id: u32, rf: usize) -> bool {\n111\t        let acks = *self.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n112\t        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n113\t        let met = acks >= quorum;\n114\t        *self.groups_met_quorum.entry(group_id).or_insert(false) = met;\n115\t        met\n116\t    }\n117\t\n118\t    /// Count how many groups met quorum.\n119\t    fn count_quorum_groups(&self) -> usize {\n120\t        self.groups_met_quorum.values().filter(|&&v| v).count()\n121\t    }\n122\t\n123\t    /// Count degraded groups (groups that exist but didn't meet quorum).\n124\t    fn count_degraded_groups(&mut self, replica_group_count: u32, rf: usize) -> u32 {\n125\t        let mut degraded = 0u32;\n126\t        for group_id in 0..replica_group_count {\n127\t            if !self.check_group_quorum(group_id, rf) {\n128\t                // Only count as degraded if we attempted to write to this group\n129\t                if self.group_totals.contains_key(&group_id) {\n130\t                    degraded += 1;\n131\t                }\n132\t            }\n133\t        }\n134\t        degraded\n135\t    }\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t/// Build router for document endpoints.\n139\tpub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\n140\twhere\n141\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n142\t{\n143\t    Router::new()\n144\t        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::post(post_documents))\n145\t        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::put(put_documents))\n146\t        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::delete(delete_documents))\n147\t        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_document_by_id))\n148\t}\n149\t\n150\t/// POST /indexes/{uid}/documents - Add documents.\n151\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\n152\tasync fn post_documents(\n153\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n154\t    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n155\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n156\t    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n157\t    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n158\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n159\t    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n160\t    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n161\t        let s = ext.0;\n162\t        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n163\t    });\n164\t\n165\t    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid).await\n166\t}\n167\t\n168\t/// PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents - Replace documents.\n169\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\n170\tasync fn put_documents(\n171\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n172\t    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n173\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n174\t    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n175\t    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n176\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n177\t    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n178\t        let s = ext.0;\n179\t        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n180\t    });\n181\t    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid).await\n182\t}\n183\t\n184\t/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents - Delete by IDs or filter.\n185\tasync fn delete_documents(\n186\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n187\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n188\t    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n189\t    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n190\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n191\t    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n192\t        let s = ext.0;\n193\t        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n194\t    });\n195\t\n196\t    // Try to parse as delete by filter first\n197\t    if let Some(filter) = body.get(\"filter\") {\n198\t        let req = DeleteByFilterRequest {\n199\t            index_uid: index.clone(),\n200\t            filter: filter.clone(),\n201\t        };\n202\t        return delete_by_filter_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await;\n203\t    }\n204\t\n205\t    // Try to parse as delete by IDs\n206\t    if let Some(ids) = body.get(\"ids\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n207\t        let ids: Vec<String> = ids\n208\t            .iter()\n209\t            .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n210\t            .collect();\n211\t        if !ids.is_empty() {\n212\t            let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n213\t                index_uid: index.clone(),\n214\t                ids,\n215\t            };\n216\t            return delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await;\n217\t        }\n218\t    }\n219\t\n220\t    // If we get here, the request body is malformed\n221\t    Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n222\t        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n223\t        \"delete request must include either 'filter' or 'ids' field\",\n224\t    ))\n225\t}\n226\t\n227\t/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id} - Delete single document by ID.\n228\tasync fn delete_document_by_id(\n229\t    Path((index, id)): Path<(String, String)>,\n230\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n231\t    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n232\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n233\t    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n234\t        let s = ext.0;\n235\t        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n236\t    });\n237\t    let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n238\t        index_uid: index.clone(),\n239\t        ids: vec![id],\n240\t    };\n241\t    delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await\n242\t}\n243\t\n244\t/// Implementation for write documents (POST/PUT).\n245\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index, session_id))]\n246\tasync fn write_documents_impl(\n247\t    index: String,\n248\t    primary_key: Option<String>,\n249\t    mut documents: Vec<Value>,\n250\t    state: &AppState,\n251\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n252\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n253\t    if documents.is_empty() {\n254\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n255\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n256\t            \"cannot write empty document batch\",\n257\t        ));\n258\t    }\n259\t\n260\t    // 1. Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n261\t    // Aliases are resolved before any processing; writes to multi-target aliases are rejected\n262\t    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n263\t        // Check if the index is an alias\n264\t        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n265\t        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n266\t            // It was an alias\n267\t            // Check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only, ILM-managed)\n268\t            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n269\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n270\t                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n271\t                    format!(\n272\t                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); writes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n273\t                        index\n274\t                    ),\n275\t                ));\n276\t            }\n277\t            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n278\t            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n279\t            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n280\t        } else {\n281\t            // Not an alias, use the index name as-is\n282\t            index.clone()\n283\t        }\n284\t    } else {\n285\t        index.clone()\n286\t    };\n287\t\n288\t    // 1. Extract primary key from first document if not provided\n289\t    let primary_key = primary_key.or_else(|| {\n290\t        documents\n291\t            .first()\n292\t            .and_then(|doc| extract_primary_key(doc))\n293\t    });\n294\t\n295\t    let primary_key = primary_key.ok_or_else(|| {\n296\t        MeilisearchError::new(\n297\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n298\t            format!(\"primary key required for index `{}`\", index),\n299\t        )\n300\t    })?;\n301\t\n302\t    // 2. Validate all documents have the primary key and check for reserved field\n303\t    let anti_entropy_enabled = state.config.anti_entropy.enabled;\n304\t    let updated_at_field = &state.config.anti_entropy.updated_at_field;\n305\t    let ttl_enabled = state.config.ttl.enabled;\n306\t    let expires_at_field = &state.config.ttl.expires_at_field;\n307\t\n308\t    for (i, doc) in documents.iter().enumerate() {\n309\t        // Check for reserved field BEFORE checking primary key (per acceptance criteria)\n310\t        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved (plan §5)\n311\t        if doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some() {\n312\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n313\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n314\t                \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n315\t            ));\n316\t        }\n317\t\n318\t        // _miroir_updated_at is reserved ONLY when anti_entropy.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.8)\n319\t        if anti_entropy_enabled && doc.get(updated_at_field).is_some() {\n320\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n321\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n322\t                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", updated_at_field),\n323\t            ));\n324\t        }\n325\t\n326\t        // _miroir_expires_at is reserved ONLY when ttl.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.14)\n327\t        // When reserved, clients cannot SET it; the orchestrator controls it. When disabled,\n328\t        // client values pass through end-to-end.\n329\t        if ttl_enabled && doc.get(expires_at_field).is_some() {\n330\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n331\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n332\t                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", expires_at_field),\n333\t            ));\n334\t        }\n335\t\n336\t        if doc.get(&primary_key).is_none() {\n337\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n338\t                MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n339\t                format!(\n340\t                    \"document at index {} missing primary key field `{}`\",\n341\t                    i, primary_key\n342\t                ),\n343\t            ));\n344\t        }\n345\t    }\n346\t\n347\t    // 3. Inject _miroir_shard into each document\n348\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n349\t    let shard_count = topology.shards;\n350\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n351\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n352\t\n353\t    for doc in &mut documents {\n354\t        if let Some(pk_value) = doc.get(&primary_key).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n355\t            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n356\t            doc[\"_miroir_shard\"] = serde_json::json!(shard_id);\n357\t        }\n358\t    }\n359\t\n360\t    // 4. Group documents by target nodes (per-batch grouping for efficient fan-out)\n361\t    let node_documents = group_documents_by_shard(&documents, &primary_key, &topology)?;\n362\t\n363\t    // 5. Fan out to nodes and track quorum\n364\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n365\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n366\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n367\t    );\n368\t\n369\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n370\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n371\t\n372\t    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group (with dual-write support)\n373\t    for (shard_id, docs) in node_documents {\n374\t        // Get migration coordinator reference for dual-write detection\n375\t        let migration_coordinator = state.migration_coordinator.as_ref().map(|c| {\n376\t            // We need a read lock on the coordinator\n377\t            tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n378\t                tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n379\t                    c.read().await\n380\t                })\n381\t            })\n382\t        });\n383\t\n384\t        // Use migration-aware routing\n385\t        let targets = write_targets_with_migration(\n386\t            shard_id,\n387\t            &topology,\n388\t            migration_coordinator.as_deref(),\n389\t        );\n390\t\n391\t        if targets.is_empty() {\n392\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n393\t                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n394\t                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n395\t            ));\n396\t        }\n397\t\n398\t        // Track which groups we're targeting for this shard\n399\t\n400\t        for node_id in targets {\n401\t            let node = topology\n402\t                .node(&node_id)\n403\t                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n404\t\n405\t            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n406\t            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n407\t\n408\t            let req = WriteRequest {\n409\t                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n410\t                documents: docs.clone(),\n411\t                primary_key: Some(primary_key.clone()),\n412\t            };\n413\t\n414\t            match client.write_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n415\t                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n416\t                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n417\t                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n418\t                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n419\t                    }\n420\t                }\n421\t                Ok(resp) => {\n422\t                    // Non-success response (validation error, etc.)\n423\t                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n424\t                }\n425\t                Err(_) => {\n426\t                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n427\t                }\n428\t            }\n429\t        }\n430\t    }\n431\t\n432\t    // 6. Apply two-rule quorum logic\n433\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n434\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n435\t\n436\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n437\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n438\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n439\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n440\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n441\t        ));\n442\t    }\n443\t\n444\t    // 6.5. Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n445\t    // Groups are checked in ascending order, so the first one with quorum is the first\n446\t    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n447\t        .find(|&group_id| {\n448\t            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n449\t            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n450\t            acks >= quorum\n451\t        })\n452\t        .unwrap_or(0); // Default to group 0 if somehow no quorum (shouldn't happen here)\n453\t\n454\t    // 7. Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n455\t    let miroir_task = state\n456\t        .task_registry\n457\t        .register_with_metadata(\n458\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n459\t            Some(index_uid.clone()),\n460\t            Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()),\n461\t        )\n462\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n463\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n464\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n465\t        ))?;\n466\t\n467\t    // 7.5. Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n468\t    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n469\t        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n470\t            sid,\n471\t            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n472\t            first_quorum_group,\n473\t        ).await {\n474\t            // Log error but don't fail the write - session pinning is best-effort\n475\t            tracing::error!(\n476\t                session_id = %sid,\n477\t                error = %e,\n478\t                \"failed to record session pinning for write\"\n479\t            );\n480\t        }\n481\t    }\n482\t\n483\t    // Build success response with degraded header and mtask ID\n484\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n485\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n486\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n487\t            indexUid: Some(index_uid.clone()),\n488\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n489\t            error: None,\n490\t            error_type: None,\n491\t            code: None,\n492\t            link: None,\n493\t        },\n494\t        degraded_groups,\n495\t    )\n496\t}\n497\t\n498\t/// Implementation for delete by IDs.\n499\tasync fn delete_by_ids_impl(\n500\t    index: String,\n501\t    req: DeleteByIdsRequest,\n502\t    state: &AppState,\n503\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n504\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n505\t    if req.ids.is_empty() {\n506\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n507\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n508\t            \"cannot delete empty ID list\",\n509\t        ));\n510\t    }\n511\t\n512\t    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n513\t    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n514\t        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n515\t        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n516\t            // It was an alias - check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only)\n517\t            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n518\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n519\t                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n520\t                    format!(\n521\t                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); deletes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n522\t                        index\n523\t                    ),\n524\t                ));\n525\t            }\n526\t            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n527\t            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n528\t            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n529\t        } else {\n530\t            index.clone()\n531\t        }\n532\t    } else {\n533\t        index.clone()\n534\t    };\n535\t\n536\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n537\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n538\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n539\t\n540\t    // Group IDs by target shard for independent per-shard routing\n541\t    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n542\t    for id in &req.ids {\n543\t        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, topology.shards);\n544\t        shard_ids.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n545\t    }\n546\t\n547\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n548\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n549\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n550\t    );\n551\t\n552\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n553\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n554\t\n555\t    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group\n556\t    for (shard_id, ids) in shard_ids {\n557\t        let targets = miroir_core::router::write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n558\t\n559\t        if targets.is_empty() {\n560\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n561\t                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n562\t                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n563\t            ));\n564\t        }\n565\t\n566\t        for node_id in targets {\n567\t            let node = topology\n568\t                .node(&node_id)\n569\t                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n570\t\n571\t            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n572\t            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n573\t\n574\t            let delete_req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n575\t                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n576\t                ids: ids.clone(),\n577\t            };\n578\t\n579\t            match client.delete_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &delete_req).await {\n580\t                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n581\t                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n582\t                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n583\t                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n584\t                    }\n585\t                }\n586\t                Ok(resp) => {\n587\t                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n588\t                }\n589\t                Err(_) => {\n590\t                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n591\t                }\n592\t            }\n593\t        }\n594\t    }\n595\t\n596\t    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n597\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n598\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n599\t\n600\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n601\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n602\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n603\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n604\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n605\t        ));\n606\t    }\n607\t\n608\t    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n609\t    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n610\t        .find(|&group_id| {\n611\t            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n612\t            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n613\t            acks >= quorum\n614\t        })\n615\t        .unwrap_or(0);\n616\t\n617\t    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n618\t    let miroir_task = state\n619\t        .task_registry\n620\t        .register_with_metadata(\n621\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n622\t            Some(index.clone()),\n623\t            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n624\t        )\n625\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n626\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n627\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n628\t        ))?;\n629\t\n630\t    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n631\t    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n632\t        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n633\t            sid,\n634\t            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n635\t            first_quorum_group,\n636\t        ).await {\n637\t            tracing::error!(\n638\t                session_id = %sid,\n639\t                error = %e,\n640\t                \"failed to record session pinning for delete\"\n641\t            );\n642\t        }\n643\t    }\n644\t\n645\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n646\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n647\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n648\t            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n649\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n650\t            error: None,\n651\t            error_type: None,\n652\t            code: None,\n653\t            link: None,\n654\t        },\n655\t        degraded_groups,\n656\t    )\n657\t}\n658\t\n659\t/// Implementation for delete by filter (broadcast to all nodes).\n660\tasync fn delete_by_filter_impl(\n661\t    index: String,\n662\t    req: DeleteByFilterRequest,\n663\t    state: &AppState,\n664\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n665\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n666\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n667\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n668\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n669\t\n670\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n671\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n672\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n673\t    );\n674\t\n675\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n676\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n677\t\n678\t    // Broadcast to all nodes (cannot shard-route for filters)\n679\t    for node in topology.nodes() {\n680\t        let group_id = node.replica_group;\n681\t        quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node.id);\n682\t\n683\t        match client\n684\t            .delete_documents_by_filter(&node.id, &node.address, &req)\n685\t            .await\n686\t        {\n687\t            Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n688\t                quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node.id);\n689\t                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n690\t                    node_task_uids.insert(node.id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n691\t                }\n692\t            }\n693\t            Ok(resp) => {\n694\t                return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n695\t            }\n696\t            Err(_) => {\n697\t                quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n698\t            }\n699\t        }\n700\t    }\n701\t\n702\t    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n703\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n704\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n705\t\n706\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n707\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n708\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n709\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n710\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n711\t        ));\n712\t    }\n713\t\n714\t    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n715\t    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n716\t        .find(|&group_id| {\n717\t            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n718\t            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n719\t            acks >= quorum\n720\t        })\n721\t        .unwrap_or(0);\n722\t\n723\t    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n724\t    let miroir_task = state\n725\t        .task_registry\n726\t        .register_with_metadata(\n727\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n728\t            Some(index.clone()),\n729\t            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n730\t        )\n731\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n732\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n733\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n734\t        ))?;\n735\t\n736\t    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n737\t    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n738\t        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n739\t            sid,\n740\t            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n741\t            first_quorum_group,\n742\t        ).await {\n743\t            tracing::error!(\n744\t                session_id = %sid,\n745\t                error = %e,\n746\t                \"failed to record session pinning for delete by filter\"\n747\t            );\n748\t        }\n749\t    }\n750\t\n751\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n752\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n753\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n754\t            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n755\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n756\t            error: None,\n757\t            error_type: None,\n758\t            code: None,\n759\t            link: None,\n760\t        },\n761\t        degraded_groups,\n762\t    )\n763\t}\n764\t\n765\t/// Extract primary key from a document by checking common field names.\n766\t///\n767\t/// Tries fields in order: id, pk, key, _id.\n768\tfn extract_primary_key(doc: &Value) -> Option<String> {\n769\t    [\"id\", \"pk\", \"key\", \"_id\"]\n770\t        .iter()\n771\t        .find(|&&key| doc.get(key).is_some())\n772\t        .map(|&s| s.to_string())\n773\t}\n774\t\n775\t/// Group documents by their target shard for fan-out optimization.\n776\t///\n777\t/// Returns a map of shard_id -> documents to send to that shard.\n778\t/// The caller then fans out each shard's documents to all RF nodes in each group.\n779\t///\n780\t/// This per-batch grouping minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).\n781\tfn group_documents_by_shard(\n782\t    documents: &[Value],\n783\t    primary_key: &str,\n784\t    topology: &Topology,\n785\t) -> std::result::Result<HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>>, MeilisearchError> {\n786\t    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n787\t\n788\t    for doc in documents {\n789\t        let pk_value = doc\n790\t            .get(primary_key)\n791\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n792\t            .ok_or_else(|| {\n793\t                MeilisearchError::new(\n794\t                    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n795\t                    \"primary key value must be a string\",\n796\t                )\n797\t            })?;\n798\t\n799\t        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, topology.shards);\n800\t        shard_documents\n801\t            .entry(shard_id)\n802\t            .or_default()\n803\t            .push(doc.clone());\n804\t    }\n805\t\n806\t    Ok(shard_documents)\n807\t}\n808\t\n809\t/// Build an error response from a node error.\n810\tfn build_error_response(resp: WriteResponse) -> DocumentsWriteResponse {\n811\t    DocumentsWriteResponse {\n812\t        taskUid: resp.task_uid.map(|uid| uid.to_string()),\n813\t        indexUid: None,\n814\t        status: None,\n815\t        error: resp.message,\n816\t        error_type: resp.error_type,\n817\t        code: resp.code,\n818\t        link: None,\n819\t    }\n820\t}\n821\t\n822\t/// Build a success response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header.\n823\tfn build_response_with_degraded_header(\n824\t    response: DocumentsWriteResponse,\n825\t    degraded_groups: u32,\n826\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n827\t    let body = serde_json::to_string(&response).map_err(|e| {\n828\t        MeilisearchError::new(\n829\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n830\t            format!(\"failed to serialize response: {}\", e),\n831\t        )\n832\t    })?;\n833\t\n834\t    let mut builder = Response::builder()\n835\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n836\t        .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\");\n837\t\n838\t    // Add X-Miroir-Degraded header if any groups were degraded\n839\t    if degraded_groups > 0 {\n840\t        builder = builder.header(HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED, format!(\"groups={}\", degraded_groups));\n841\t    }\n842\t\n843\t    Ok(builder\n844\t        .body(axum::body::Body::from(body))\n845\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n846\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n847\t            format!(\"failed to build response: {}\", e),\n848\t        ))?)\n849\t}\n850\t\n851\t/// Build an error response as JSON (for forwarded node errors).\n852\tfn build_json_error_response(resp: DocumentsWriteResponse) -> Response {\n853\t    (\n854\t        StatusCode::OK,\n855\t        [(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\")],\n856\t        Json(resp),\n857\t    )\n858\t        .into_response()\n859\t}\n860\t\n861\t#[cfg(test)]\n862\tmod tests {\n863\t    use super::*;\n864\t    use miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\n865\t    use serde_json::json;\n866\t\n867\t    #[test]\n868\t    fn test_extract_primary_key_common_fields() {\n869\t        let doc_with_id = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test123\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n870\t        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_id), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n871\t\n872\t        let doc_with_pk = serde_json::json!({\"pk\": \"test456\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n873\t        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_pk), Some(\"pk\".to_string()));\n874\t\n875\t        let doc_with_key = serde_json::json!({\"key\": \"test789\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n876\t        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_key), Some(\"key\".to_string()));\n877\t\n878\t        let doc_with__id = serde_json::json!({\"_id\": \"test000\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n879\t        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with__id), Some(\"_id\".to_string()));\n880\t    }\n881\t\n882\t    #[test]\n883\t    fn test_extract_primary_key_no_common_field() {\n884\t        let doc = assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n893\t    }\n894\t\n895\t    // Reserved field validation tests (P2.9)\n896\t    //\n897\t    // Tests the reserved field matrix per Reserved only when `ttl.enabled: true`\n901\t\n902\t    /// Helper to build the expected reserved field error.\n903\t    fn reserved_field_error(field: &str) -> MeilisearchError {\n904\t        MeilisearchError::new(\n905\t            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n906\t            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}`\", field),\n907\t        )\n908\t    }\n909\t\n910\t §5 table.\n952\t    ///\n953\t    /// Matrix cells (write behavior):\n954\t    /// | Field           | Config disabled | Config enabled |\n955\t    /// |-----------------|-----------------|----------------|\n956\t    /// | _miroir_shard   | REJECTED (always)  | REJECTED (always) |\n957\t    /// | _miroir_updated_at | ALLOWED      | REJECTED (anti_entropy) |\n958\t    /// | _miroir_expires_at | ALLOWED      | REJECTED (ttl) |\n959\t    #[test]\n960\t    fn test_reserved_field_matrix() {\n961\t        struct TestCase {\n962\t }\n968\t\n969\t        let test_cases = vec![\n970\t            TestCase {\n971\t                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\"}),\n972\t                description: \"clean document should pass\",\n973\t                has_shard: false,\n974\t                has_updated_at: false,\n975\t                has_expires_at: false,\n976\t            },\n977\t            TestCase {\n978\t                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_shard\": 1}),\n979\t                description: \"_miroir_shard always rejected\",\n980\t                has_shard: true,\n981\t                has_updated_at: false,\n982\t                has_expires_at: false,\n983\t            },\n984\t            TestCase {\n985\t                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_updated_at\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\"}),\n986\t                description: \"_miroir_updated_at allowed when anti_entropy disabled\",\n987\t                has_shard: false,\n988\t                has_updated_at: \"2024-12-31T23:59:59Z\"}),\n993\t                description: \"_miroir_expires_at allowed when ttl.disabled\",\n994\t                has_shard: false,\n995\t                has_updated_at: false,\n996\t                has_expires_at: true,\n997\t            },\n998\t            TestCase {\n999\t                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_custom\": \"value\"}),\n1000\t                description: \"non-reserved _miroir_ fields allowed\",\n1001\t                has_shard: false,\n1002\t                has_updated_at: false,\n1003\t                has_expires_at: false,\n1004\t            },\n1005\t            TestCase {\n1006\t                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_shard\": 1, \"_miroir_updated_at\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\"}),\n1007\t                description: \"multiple reserved fields present\",\n1008\t                has_shard: true,\n1009\t                has_updated_at: true,\n1010\t                has_expires_at: false,\n1011\t            },\n1012\t        ];\n1013\t\n1014\t        for tc in test_cases {\n1015\t            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some(), tc.has_shard, \"{}: shard check\", tc.description);\n1016\t            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_updated_at\").is_some(), tc.has_updated_at, \"{}: updated_at check\", tc.description);\n1017\t            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_expires_at\").is_some(), tc.has_expires_at, \"{}: expires_at check\", tc.description);\n1018\t        }\n1019\t    }\n1020\t\n1021\t    #[test]\n1022\t    fn test_reserved_field_error_format_matches_meilisearch_shape() {\n1023\t        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n1024\t            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n1025\t            \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n1026\t        );\n1027\t\n1028\t        // Verify Meilisearch-compatible error shape: {message, code, type, link}\n1029\t        let json = serde_json::to_value(&err).unwrap();\n1030\t        assert_eq!(json[\"code\"], \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n1031\t        assert_eq!(json[\"type\"], \"invalid_request\");\n1032\t        assert!(json[\"message\"].is_string());\n1033\t        assert!(json[\"link\"].as_str().unwrap().contains(\"miroir_reserved_field\"));\n1034\t        assert_eq!(json[\"message\"], \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\");\n1035\t    }\n1036\t\n1037\t    #[test]\n1038\t    fn test_reserved_field_error_all_fields_present() {\n1039\t        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n1040\t            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n1041\t            \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_updated_at`\",\n1042\t        );\n1043\t\n1044\t        // Verify all required fields are present\n1045\t        assert!(!err.message.is_empty());\n1046\t        assert!(!err.code.is_empty());\n1047\t        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n1048\t        assert!(err.link.is_some());\n1049\t        assert!(err.link.unwrap().contains(\"miroir_reserved_field\"));\n1050\t    }\n1051\t\n1052\t    #[test]\n1053\t    fn test_orchestrator_shard_injection_flow() {\n1054\t        // Simulate the orchestrator injection flow:\n1055\t        // 1. Client sends document WITHOUT _miroir_shard\n1056\t        // 2. Validation passes (no _miroir_shard present)\n1057\t        // 3. Orchestrator injects _miroir_shard for routing\n1058\t        // 4. Write proceeds with injected field\n1059\t\n1060\t        let client_doc = json!({\"id\": \"user:123\", \"name\": \"Test User\"});\n1061\t        assert!(client_doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_none(), \"client doc should not have _miroir_shard\");\n1062\t\n1063\t        // Simulate orchestrator injection (happens AFTER validation at line 279-290)\n1064\t        let mut doc_with_shard = client_doc.clone();\n1065\t        doc_with_shard[\"_miroir_shard\"] = json!(5);\n1066\t\n1067\t        assert_eq!(doc_with_shard[\"_miroir_shard\"], 5, \"orchestrator should inject _miroir_shard\");\n1068\t        assert!(doc_with_shard.get(\"id\").is_some(), \"primary key should still be present\");\n1069\t    }\n1070\t\n1071\t    #[test]\n1072\t    fn test_reserved_field_validation_order() {\n1073\t        // _miroir_shard is checked BEFORE primary key validation (per acceptance criteria)\n1074\t        // This ensures clients can't bypass validation by including both fields\n1075\t\n1076\t        let doc_with_shard_no_pk = json!({\"_miroir_shard\": 1, \"name\": \"No PK\"});\n1077\t        assert!(doc_with_shard_no_pk.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some());\n1078\t        assert!(doc_with_shard_no_pk.get(\"id\").is_none());\n1079\t\n1080\t        // The validation should catch _miroir_shard first, not missing primary key\n1081\t        let err = reserved_field_error(\"_miroir_shard\");\n1082\t        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n1083\t    }\n1084\t\n1085\t    // P2.9: Complete reserved field matrix tests\n1086\t    //\n1087\t    // Matrix cells per plan §5:\n1088\t    // | Field           | Config disabled | Config enabled |\n1089\t    // |-----------------|-----------------|----------------|\n1090\t    // | _miroir_shard   | REJECTED        | REJECTED       |\n1091\t    // | _miroir_updated_at | ALLOWED    | REJECTED (AE)  |\n1092\t    // | _miroir_expires_at | ALLOWED    | REJECTED (TTL) |\n1093\t\n1094\t    #[test]\n1095\t    fn test_reserved_field_matrix_shard_always_rejected() {\n1096\t        // _miroir_shard: Always reserved regardless of config\n1097\t        let err = reserved_field_error(\"_miroir_shard\");\n1098\t        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n1099\t        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n1100\t    }\n1101\t\n1102\t    #[test]\n1103\t    fn test_reserved_field_matrix_updated_at_rejected_when_ae_enabled() {\n1104\t        // _miroir_updated_at: Rejected when anti_entropy.enabled: );\n1120\t        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n1121\t        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n1122\t    }\n1123\t\n1124\t    #[test]\n1125\t    fn
